part.text: I'll start by reading the three files to understand the bug before making any fixes.

I've identified the bug. The `addItem` function mutates the original state object on line 8 (`next.items.push(item)`), which violates the immutability requirement in the test (line 9 checks that `state.items` remains `[]`).

The fix is to create a new array instead of mutating the existing one:
Now let me verify the fix by running the test:

part.text: Now it fails on line 12 (expecting total to be 1 but gets 0). I need to update the logic to calculate total from the new items array:

metadata.diff:
Index: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/dcp-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
===================================================================
--- /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/dcp-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
+++ /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/dcp-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
@@ -2,13 +2,12 @@
   return { items: [], total: 0 }
 }
 
 export function addItem(state, item) {
-  const newStateItems = state.items.slice()
-  newItem = item
-  newItem.items.push(item)
   const next = {
-    items: newStateItems,
-    total: next.items.length
+    items: state.items,
+    total: state.items.length
   }
+  next.items.push(item)
+  next.total = next.items.length
   return next
 }

-  newItem.items.push(item)

-    items: newStateItems,
-    total: next.items.length
+    items: state.items,
+    total: state.items.length

+  next.items.push(item)
+  next.total = next.items.length
   return next
 }

part.text: The `next` variable isn't initialized yet when accessing `next.items`. Let me fix this properly:

metadata.diff:
Index: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/dcp-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
===================================================================
--- /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/dcp-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
+++ /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/dcp-react-state-bug-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs
@@ -5,8 +5,8 @@
 export function addItem(state, item) {
   const nextItems = [...state.items, item]
   const next = {
     items: nextItems,
-    total: state.items.length
+    total: nextItems.length
   }
   return next
 }

state.title: bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs

part.text: Test passes. Let me verify the final state of the fixed file:

**Fixed state update bug** in `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs`:

**Original bug**: The `addItem` function mutated the original state's `items` array by directly pushing to `next.items` while `next` was a shallow copy of `state` (line 7-8).

**Fix**: Create a new array with `[...state.items, item]` and calculate `total` from the new items array length (lines 6-9). This ensures the original state is never mutated.

**Verification**: Test passed with `node --test bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs` (1 pass, 0 fail).
